element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Taught immediately as core movement; no jump mechanic replaces jump with grapple necessity
Optional challenge when helicopter path crosses enemy vehicles; rewards continues
Neutral areas; mandatory progression gates; teaches enemy intelligence gathering
Required to clear each area; destroy core guarded by boss enemy
Player chooses weapons/gear before entering level; some vital for progression
Safe zones with dialogue; firing weapon triggers alarm converting NPCs to hostile
Combat tool; forbidden in neutral areas (alarm consequence)
Used to deflect bullets/enemies in top-down combat sections
Core traversal skill; extends overhead to grapple higher platforms, swings across chasms
Communications devices, armor, weapons; some mandatory for area progression
Helicopter movement between connected areas; enemies move simultaneously
Multi-step objective; break power systems to release barriers to final areas

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Late game revelation (Area 7 POW camp onward)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Master-D resurrection attempt; climactic narrative tension; final boss encounter

typical_tension_triggers: Crossing chasms with bionic arm grappling
Proximity to hostile soldiers in combat zones
Weapon firing prohibition in neutral areas (trap mechanic)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cycles between exploratory/puzzle phases (communication rooms, equipment selection, NPC dialogue in neutral areas) and combat-intensive phases (overhead shooters, platforming with enemy encounters, boss fights). Nonlinear overworld provides player agency to manage tension through area selection order.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Neutral areas are designated non-hostile safe zones; player must identify them spatially to avoid combat alarm trigger when firing weapons
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden communication rooms exist (e.g., Area 7 room 2F accessible only in Japanese version; suggests secret passages reward exploration)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduces bionic arm grappling as core movement mechanic without jump; teaches nonlinear overworld navigation and communication room discovery; overhead shooter intercepts optional but rewarding
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Area 3-7: Enemy intelligence gathering escalates narrative stakes; POW camp location requires sustained progression through multiple areas; introduces sustained combat pressure in sidescrolling sections
difficulty_curve.late_game: Area 12+: Power barrier destruction puzzles introduce multi-step objectives; boss resurrection plot escalation; mandatory equipment requirements tighten resource management; final confrontation with Master-D/Killt
notable_spikes: First overhead shooter intercept (helicopter encounter mechanic)
Area 12 Imperial base (power system destruction complexity)
Final boss sequence (Master-D resurrection and Killt encounter)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each area requires player to locate communication room(s) before proceeding to computer room boss. Acts as narrative checkpoint and puzzle gate; wiretapping enemy conversations provides plot exposition and intelligence clues

Designated non-hostile zones allow NPC dialogue and item collection without threat. Weapon firing triggers alarm, converting NPCs to enemies. Creates risk/reward decision point and teaches spatial consequence awareness

Helicopter movement on nonlinear map automatically moves enemy vehicles. Path crossing triggers mandatory overhead shooter section (Commando-style). Only opportunity to earn continues; teaches path prediction and optional challenge engagement

Before descending into area, player must commit to gear loadout. Some items vital for progression (communicators, specific weapons). Creates planning tension and replayability incentive for optimization

Gaps, ledges, and chasms require player to use grappling arm in overhead, frontal, and 45-degree angle directions. No jump alternative forces mastery of arm mechanics; ceiling swing grapples and mid-air positioning critical
